creator: linimon@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->suspended

This is the main reason why perl was evicted from the base system during 
the 5.X development cycle. 

There are several solutions that need to be investigated (e.g. some logic 
like that is used in bsd.gcc.mk where you can state dependencies on "gcc 
later than 3.2", and The Right Thing happens).  This would fix the 5.6/5.8 
problem. 

But the fact is that 4.X is nearing EOL (January 31, 2007) and most of the 
people adding Perl ports are no longer testing on 4.X.  The latest 4.X 
port cluster build was very incomplete because of this.  While it's true 
that some workaround is needed, the reality is that with 6 months to go, 
it is very unlikely that all of these hundreds of ports are going to be 
individually fixed to work with perl5.003. 

I'm going to mark this as 'suspended' awaiting some interested individual 
to attempt one or the other of these subtasks.
